opp.ais_why:
• Per the 20 Apr 2026 rep note: 'Positive exec engagement with clear needs expressed around S2C+Ag AI (GEP remains to cover P2P). Understand Zycus unique value with focus on single system with strong AI capabilities. A serie of Workshops in coming weeks. Objective to make a decision in Sep.' Buyer (G CPO Jay Taylor and team) has explicitly framed needs as 'S2C + Agentic AI' with focus on 'single system with strong AI capabilities'.
• Per the 27 Apr 2026 note: 'Confirmed engagement by mid-May. Currently working on internal AI-Roadmap with Zycus AI capabilities in mind.' Per 30 Jan: 'AI-Session with Antoine Le Flamanc (Head IT Proc). Challenges on Sourcing & eProc. Mention GEP to be renewed by mid 2027. If Zycus can generate positive AI-Business case as quick wins, window of opportunity to get Zycus S2C live by date of GEP renewal (FY26 opp).' Buyer is actively building an internal AI Roadmap with Zycus AI capabilities specifically in mind, and an AI business-case is the gating criterion.
• Confidence is High because there is buyer-stated 'S2C + Agentic AI' need, an explicit AI-Roadmap building with Zycus capabilities, an AI-business-case gating criterion, named CPO + named Head IT Proc stakeholders, and a workshops-to-September decision plan. Product scope includes Merlin Intake, Agentic AI and ANA. Caveats: GEP P2P remains in place (split-vendor scenario), the decision is in September. Score 9 reflects explicit buyer-led AI demand, AI as gating criterion, and active multi-stakeholder workshop cadence.

sources: Next Step, 30 Jan 2026: AI-Session with Antoine Le Flamanc, challenges on Sourcing & eProc, GEP renewal mid-2027, need for quick-win AI business case

metrics.narrative: DFI articulated operational pain around sourcing and eProcurement challenges during the 30 January AI session with the relevant stakeholder (Head of IT Procurement), noting GEP renewal mid-2027 and seeking quick-win AI business case to justify Zycus S2C live by GEP renewal date. The buyer expressed need for capacity recovery through conversational intake (Jacqueline: system should respond with explanations rather than requiring navigation of complex menus, per Next Step). However, no quantified metrics have been documented: no cost savings target, headcount recovery number, cycle-time reduction, or error-rate improvement. The business case remains qualitative (AI capabilities, single-system simplicity, GEP dissatisfaction) rather than anchored to a financial ROI model. Quantifying the capacity-recovery impact and building a joint ROI model is the critical next move to secure Economic Buyer engagement and justify the $360K investment.

champion.narrative: Jacqueline Tuason (Group Procurement Process Manager) is the confirmed champion. She has consistently advocated for Zycus since 26 March, confirming interest to go deeper (26 Mar, 2 Apr), engaging at the Manila event (2 Apr), and most recently confirming on 27 April that DFI is working on an internal AI-Roadmap with the relevant stakeholder capabilities in mind, signaling active internal advocacy. She has direct access to Jay Taylor (the Decision Maker) and is driving the evaluation process day-to-day. Her engagement is active as of late April; the 6 June rep outreach was to follow up with both Jay and Jacqueline, suggesting continued two-way dialogue. However, Jacqueline does not control budget or final decision authority, and her power is limited to process influence and DM access. The champion strength is strong within the evaluation phase but will need to escalate to Economic Buyer sponsorship to carry the deal through commercials and contract negotiation. The risk is single-threading: Jacqueline and Jay are the only active buyers in the last 60 days; Antoine (Head of IT Procurement) has not been engaged since 30 January (5 months ago), and no CFO or finance stakeholders are mapped despite Kevinson Obrero (Procurement Finance Manager) being in the contact roles.

competition.narrative: DFI is actively evaluating Zycus against two competitors: GEP Worldwide (the incumbent) and Coupa. GEP is the primary competitive threat and the incumbent, covering P2P but not S2C + Agentic AI (per 20 Apr Next Step), with contract renewal mid-2027. Jay Taylor confirmed on 18 March that DFI is not happy with GEP and may look for replacement, creating the displacement window. However, GEP's incumbency is a defensive advantage (switching cost, integration depth, relationship inertia), and the mid-2027 renewal date means GEP will fight to retain. Coupa is listed in the Salesforce Competitor field but has not been mentioned in any Next Step entry or known buyer conversation, suggesting it may be a placeholder or early-stage consideration rather than an active head-to-head. The wedge against GEP is clear: Zycus offers a single unified system covering S2C + P2P + Agentic AI with strong AI capabilities, versus GEP's fragmented coverage and weaker AI story. The activation is to make Agentic AI (Merlin Intake, autonomous sourcing) the Phase 1 centerpiece in the August-September proof-of-value, demonstrating immediate capacity recovery and quick-win ROI that GEP cannot match, and positioning Zycus as the AI-forward platform aligned with DFI's internal AI-Roadmap (per 27 Apr).

sources: Next Step, 18 Mar: Jay Taylor confirmed DFI not happy with GEP
Next Step, 20 Apr: GEP covers P2P but not S2C + Agentic AI, creating coverage gap

identify_pain.narrative: DFI's pain is two-fold and clearly articulated. First, operational capacity constraints in procurement: Jacqueline (Champion, Group Procurement Process Manager) expressed need for a conversational intake system that responds with explanations rather than forcing users to navigate complex menus, signaling procurement team bandwidth strain and user-friction pain (per Next Step and known commitments). Second, strategic dissatisfaction with GEP: Jay Taylor confirmed DFI is not happy with GEP (18 Mar Next Step), and GEP's coverage gap (P2P only, does not cover S2C + Agentic AI per 20 Apr) is forcing a replacement evaluation. The pain owner is Jay Taylor (Group CPO, Decision Maker) who is driving the evaluation with Jacqueline as the process owner and champion. Antoine (Head of IT Procurement) raised the AI quick-win business case requirement on 30 Jan, positioning him as the IT-side pain owner. The pain is confirmed and owned at senior procurement and IT leadership, but the financial/CFO pain (budget impact, cost-center pressure) has not been articulated, which is why the Economic Buyer remains unmapped.

sources: Next Step, 15 Apr: autonomous sourcing trial pending cybersecurity due diligence, 6-8 week timeline
Next Step, 30 Jan: GEP renewal mid-2027 (contract expiration forcing function)
Inference: December close with year-end implementation requires InfoSec and legal to run in parallel with evaluation, not sequentially
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics have not been detailed. The 15 April Next Step mentions autonomous sourcing trial pending cybersecurity due diligence (6-8 weeks from mid-April, so expected by early June), signaling that InfoSec review is a gate, but the InfoSec approval process, timeline, and stakeholders are not documented. No mention of MSA, SOW, legal redlining process, or procurement channel (direct vs partner-led). GEP's contract expires mid-2027, creating a forcing function for Zycus to go live by then, but no replacement contract structure or negotiation timeline has been discussed. Implementation scoping and partner involvement (SI or direct) are also undefined. The paper process is a known risk: with a December close and year-end implementation target, InfoSec, legal, and contracting must start in parallel with the proof-of-value phase (August), not after decision (September). The recommended move is to surface the paper-process timeline with Jay in the next two weeks and identify the InfoSec, legal, and procurement contacts who will drive it.

sources: Contact Roles: Jay Taylor = Decision Maker, but no Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor role assigned
Next Step series (Jan-Jun 2026): no reference to CFO, CEO, or C-level budget authority
Inference: $360K multi-year SaaS + Agentic AI + services investment in enterprise retail context requires C-level or CFO approval
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. Jay Taylor (Group Non-Trade Procurement Director) holds the Decision Maker contact role and is driving the evaluation, but for a $360K multi-year platform investment replacing GEP across Source-to-Contract plus Agentic AI, final budget authority almost certainly rests with a CFO or C-level executive. The Next Step history references procurement-level stakeholders (Jay, Jacqueline, Antoine, Kevinson in Finance) but no mention of CFO, CEO, or C-suite budget sponsor. Without EB mapping, the deal cannot progress to commercials or proposal with confidence. The recommended move is to ask Jay directly who controls the budget approval for a $360K procurement platform investment and secure an introduction to that executive within the next two weeks, ideally before the proof-of-value exercise is scheduled.

sources: Next Step series (Jan-Jun 2026): chronological engagement progression from AI session to exec engagement to workshop planning
Next Step, 20 Apr: decision objective Sep 2026, series of workshops planned
Contact Roles: Jay Taylor = Decision Maker, but no EB or approval-chain detail
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is running as a structured vendor evaluation with a September 2026 decision target. The sequence so far: (1) Initial AI session with Antoine (Head of IT Procurement) on 30 Jan established the GEP replacement opportunity; (2) Jay Taylor (Group CPO/Decision Maker) engaged 18 Mar and confirmed detailed review, unhappy with GEP; (3) Jacqueline (Champion) confirmed deeper engagement 26 Mar and 2 Apr; (4) Procurement team meetings in Hong Kong mid-April (per 13 Apr Next Step); (5) Positive exec engagement 20 Apr with workshops planned; (6) Mid-May engagement confirmation (27 Apr). The planned gates ahead are: proof-of-value workshops in August-September, decision in September, contract negotiation, InfoSec/legal review, and close by December to allow implementation by year-end. However, the approval chain is not yet mapped: Jay is the Decision Maker but the Economic Buyer (CFO or C-suite) has not been identified, and we do not know whether procurement, finance, legal, and InfoSec approvals run in sequence or parallel. Clarifying the approval workflow and timeline is critical to defending the December close date.

sources: Next Step, 30 Jan: quick-win AI business case, GEP renewal forcing function

decision_criteria.narrative: DFI's evaluation criteria are clear from the engagement history: (1) AI capabilities, specifically agentic AI for sourcing and conversational intake to recover procurement capacity (articulated 30 Jan and 20 Apr, reiterated 27 Apr as internal AI-Roadmap alignment); (2) single unified system replacing GEP's fragmented coverage (GEP covers P2P but not S2C+Agentic AI, per 20 Apr); (3) proof-of-value against specific category pain points, to be validated in August-September workshops (20 Apr: series of workshops planned, objective to decide in Sep); (4) implementation readiness to go live by GEP renewal mid-2027 (30 Jan forcing function). The criteria are confirmed but the formal scoring framework, weighting, and test-case scenarios for the proof-of-value have not yet been documented. Locking these down in writing with Jay and Jacqueline before the workshop is the next gate.

reasons.detail: DFI Retail Group ($360K, Formal Evaluation, replacing incumbent GEP with Zycus S2C + Agentic AI) shows zero activities, calls, or movements in the 14-day window and 22 days since any touch as of Jul 6. north_star_verdict.headline: Decision target September 2026 is 85 days out, but 14 days of silence since last buyer contact. Proof-of-value exercise remains unscheduled and Economic Buyer still unmapped. Champion Jacqueline and DM Jay engaged through mid-May but no forward progress on critical.

flags: Map the Economic Buyer (CFO or C-level budget authority) before commercials. Jay Taylor is the confirmed Decision Maker, but a $360K multi-year S2C platform investment displacing an incumbent typically requires CFO or C-suite sign-off. No named CFO, CEO, or executive sponsor has appeared in calls or contact roles. Engage Jay to confirm the approval chain and secure access to the Economic Buyer before pricing discussions begin (expected post-September decision). This is the single highest-leverage move to de-risk the commercial phase.

Deliver the autonomous sourcing trial and proof-of-value as committed (trial by early June 2026, proof-of-value August-September 2026) and make them the centerpiece of the win strategy. The buyer is prioritizing tail spend automation to recover capacity lost in the 40% team reduction, and the trial is the validation gate. Zycus is positioned as the agentic AI leader versus GEP's bolt-on approach; the proof-of-value must demonstrate the ROI and quick-win business case the Head of IT Procurement flagged on 30 January 2026 as the condition for winning the mid-2027 GEP displacement window.

Multi-thread beyond Jacqueline and Jay by engaging the Head of IT Procurement and any CFO/finance stakeholders. Jacqueline is a strong champion and Jay is the Decision Maker, but the cross-functional S2C scope (intake, sourcing, contract, risk, Merlin AI) and the capacity-recovery narrative touch IT, finance, and operations. Expand the stakeholder map to include the Head of IT Procurement (engaged 30 January 2026) and finance leads to build broader sponsorship and de-risk the approval chain before the September decision.

best_practice_check.summary: Single-threaded risk: Jacqueline (Champion) and Jay (Decision Maker) are the only active buyers in the last 60 days; Antoine (Head of IT Procurement) has not been engaged since 30 January (5 months ago), and no CFO or finance stakeholders are mapped despite Kevinson Obrero (Procurement Finance Manager) in contact roles. Economic Buyer not identified for $360K investment. Proof-of-value exercise planned for August-September but not yet scheduled with 85 days to decision gate.

competitive_position.summary: Competing primarily against GEP Worldwide (incumbent, P2P coverage, mid-2027 renewal) and Coupa (listed but not actively mentioned in buyer conversations). DFI is dissatisfied with GEP and sees Zycus as the AI-forward single-system alternative. Wedge is Agentic AI + S2C coverage + unified platform; activation is proof-of-value showcasing Merlin Intake and autonomous sourcing capacity recovery.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: DFI not happy with GEP and may look for replacement. GEP remains to cover P2P (does not cover S2C + Agentic AI).

ai_positioning_strength.score: strong, positioned but not yet validated
ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is strong and central to the deal. DFI is building an internal AI-Roadmap with Zycus AI capabilities in mind (27 Apr), and the evaluation is focused on S2C + Agentic AI (Merlin Intake, autonomous sourcing, ANA) as the differentiator vs GEP. The 30 Jan AI session with Antoine established the AI quick-win business case as the entry wedge. However, the AI narrative has not yet been quantified (no ROI model) or demonstrated in a proof-of-value, so it remains positioned but not validated.

items.quote: If Zycus can generate positive AI-Business case as quick wins, window of opportunity to get Zycus S2C live by date of GEP renewal. Working on internal AI-Roadmap with Zycus AI capabilities in mind.
items.criterion: AI capabilities and quick-win business case

items.quote: Understand Zycus unique value with focus on single system with strong AI capabilities. GEP remains to cover P2P (does not cover S2C + Agentic AI).
items.criterion: Single unified system covering S2C + P2P + Agentic AI

items.quote: System should respond to Jacqueline with explanations rather than requiring her to navigate complex menus.
items.source: Known commitment and Next Step context

items.quote: AI immediate focus with opportunity to replace GEP and to start implementation by end of 26. GEP to be renewed by mid 2027.
items.criterion: Implementation timeline to displace GEP by mid-2027

customer_expectations_fit.summary: DFI's expectations align well with Zycus strengths: AI-first platform (Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) matches their internal AI-Roadmap; single unified S2C system addresses GEP's coverage gap; capacity-recovery through conversational intake matches Jacqueline's need. Risk is timeline: September decision with December close and year-end implementation to meet mid-2027 GEP displacement is aggressive and will require fast-tracked InfoSec and contracting.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the 20 Apr 2026 rep note: 'Positive exec engagement with clear needs expressed around S2C+Ag AI (GEP remains to cover P2P). Understand Zycus unique value with focus on single system with strong AI capabilities. A serie of Workshops in coming weeks. Objective to make a decision in Sep.' Buyer (G CPO Jay Taylor and team) has explicitly framed needs as 'S2C + Agentic AI' with focus on 'single system with strong AI capabilities'.
• Per the 27 Apr 2026 note: 'Confirmed engagement by mid-May. Currently working on internal AI-Roadmap with Zycus AI capabilities in mind.' Per 30 Jan: 'AI-Session with Antoine Le Flamanc (Head IT Proc). Challenges on Sourcing & eProc. Mention GEP to be renewed by mid 2027. If Zycus can generate positive AI-Business case as quick wins, window of opportunity to get Zycus S2C live by date of GEP renewal (FY26 opp).' Buyer is actively building an internal AI Roadmap with Zycus AI capabilities specifically in mind, and an AI business-case is the gating criterion.
• Confidence is High because there is buyer-stated 'S2C + Agentic AI' need, an explicit AI-Roadmap building with Zycus capabilities, an AI-business-case gating criterion, named CPO + named Head IT Proc stakeholders, and a workshops-to-September decision plan. Product scope includes Merlin Intake, Agentic AI and ANA. Caveats: GEP P2P remains in place (split-vendor scenario), the decision is in September. Score 9 reflects explicit buyer-led AI demand, AI as gating criterion, and active multi-stakeholder workshop cadence.

Map the Economic Buyer (CFO or C-level budget authority) before commercials. Jay Taylor is the confirmed Decision Maker, but a $360K multi-year S2C platform investment displacing an incumbent typically requires CFO or C-suite sign-off. No named CFO, CEO, or executive sponsor has appeared in calls or contact roles. Engage Jay to confirm the approval chain and secure access to the Economic Buyer before pricing discussions begin (expected post-September decision). This is the single highest-leverage move to de-risk the commercial phase.

Deliver the autonomous sourcing trial and proof-of-value as committed (trial by early June 2026, proof-of-value August-September 2026) and make them the centerpiece of the win strategy. The buyer is prioritizing tail spend automation to recover capacity lost in the 40% team reduction, and the trial is the validation gate. Zycus is positioned as the agentic AI leader versus GEP's bolt-on approach; the proof-of-value must demonstrate the ROI and quick-win business case the Head of IT Procurement flagged on 30 January 2026 as the condition for winning the mid-2027 GEP displacement window.

Multi-thread beyond Jacqueline and Jay by engaging the Head of IT Procurement and any CFO/finance stakeholders. Jacqueline is a strong champion and Jay is the Decision Maker, but the cross-functional S2C scope (intake, sourcing, contract, risk, Merlin AI) and the capacity-recovery narrative touch IT, finance, and operations. Expand the stakeholder map to include the Head of IT Procurement (engaged 30 January 2026) and finance leads to build broader sponsorship and de-risk the approval chain before the September decision.

value.flag: Map the Economic Buyer (CFO or C-level budget authority) before commercials. Jay Taylor is the confirmed Decision Maker, but a $360K multi-year S2C platform investment displacing an incumbent typically requires CFO or C-suite sign-off. No named CFO, CEO, or executive sponsor has appeared in calls or contact roles. Engage Jay to confirm the approval chain and secure access to the Economic Buyer before pricing discussions begin (expected post-September decision). This is the single highest-leverage move to de-risk the commercial phase.

value.flag: Deliver the autonomous sourcing trial and proof-of-value as committed (trial by early June 2026, proof-of-value August-September 2026) and make them the centerpiece of the win strategy. The buyer is prioritizing tail spend automation to recover capacity lost in the 40% team reduction, and the trial is the validation gate. Zycus is positioned as the agentic AI leader versus GEP's bolt-on approach; the proof-of-value must demonstrate the ROI and quick-win business case the Head of IT Procurement flagged on 30 January 2026 as the condition for winning the mid-2027 GEP displacement window.

value.flag: Multi-thread beyond Jacqueline and Jay by engaging the Head of IT Procurement and any CFO/finance stakeholders. Jacqueline is a strong champion and Jay is the Decision Maker, but the cross-functional S2C scope (intake, sourcing, contract, risk, Merlin AI) and the capacity-recovery narrative touch IT, finance, and operations. Expand the stakeholder map to include the Head of IT Procurement (engaged 30 January 2026) and finance leads to build broader sponsorship and de-risk the approval chain before the September decision.
